evidenceTypesSupplied: Local derived visible-trait evidence for HERC2/OCA2, MC1R, and EDAR.
Public Skin Genes sample PDF rows used as observed output-format examples.
Consumer education references for pigmentation, hair, trait-test limits, and source binding.
sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated marketplace detail-page parity is still pending.
No calibrated skin-trait prediction model is supplied with this fixture.
Skin type, UV sensitivity, skincare response, acne, skin aging, dermatology outcomes, and skin-cancer risk are not reported.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived HERC2/OCA2 rs12913832 AG call is available for pigmentation and eye-color context. This is not an exact eye-color, skin-tone, skin-type, ancestry, identity, or dermatology result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: OCA2 and HERC2 are important in pigmentation biology, but visible traits are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived MC1R rs1805007 CT call is available for pigmentation, hair-color, and freckle context. This is not dermatology guidance, skincare guidance, or a skin-cancer risk result.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: MC1R is a well-studied pigmentation gene, but many genes and non-genetic factors contribute to hair color, freckles, and related visible traits.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived EDAR hair-texture/thickness context row is available. This is educational context only and does not predict exact hair texture or any skin-health outcome.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Hair texture and thickness can have genetic contributions, but many genes and non-genetic factors can influence the trait.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: MMP1 common GG, sample del/del; MMP3 common del/del, sample del/del; risk REDUCED; protective effect against wrinkle formation, sagging, and skin aging
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local skincare conclusion.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: FLG common/sample rows GG, TT, AA, CC; AQP3 common AA, sample GG; risk ELEVATED; tendency for lowered skin lubrication, dryness, sun-radiation sensitivity, and skin allergies
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; recommendation details are sample content, not personal hydration or skincare advice.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: AGER common GG, sample GA; risk ELEVATED; increased glycation processes with added risk for wrinkles, sagging, and loss of radiance
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local glycation or dermatology finding.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: ICAM1 AA, IL6 GG, IL1RN TT/TT, and IL1RL1 common GG, sample AA; risk ELEVATED; elevated IgE antibodies and increased inflammatory response of the skin
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local inflammation diagnosis.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: SLC45A2 common CC, sample CC; risk NORMAL; no genetic risk addition
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local dark-spot or dermatology conclusion.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: DLGAP1 common GG, sample GC; risk NORMAL; average rate of loss of elasticity around the eyes
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from page 3 of the public Skin Genes sample PDF summary table; this is observed sample output structure, not a local eyelid or dermatology conclusion.

unavailableTraits: Skin type, UV sensitivity, skin aging, acne, skincare product response, and dermatology outcomes are unavailable and not inferred.
Sample PDF lifestyle, diet, and cosmetics recommendation text is not converted into personal advice.
Skin pigmentation and freckles are limited to educational pigmentation context, not skin-cancer or medical-risk interpretation.
plainEnglishLimits: Visible traits are influenced by multiple genes and non-genetic factors.
Direct-to-consumer trait tests provide estimates or context, not certainty about exact appearance.
Public sample rows show observed report format and source binding; they are not local personal findings unless the local run supplies equivalent validated evidence.
This report must not be used to identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, guide skincare, or report disease risk.

references.title: MedlinePlus Genetics: Direct-to-consumer genetic test types

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample summary table uses MMP1 in the Elasticity row.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local wrinkle, sagging, skin-aging, or skincare actionability without validated evidence.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample summary table uses MMP3 in the Elasticity row.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; keep separate from local visible-trait marker evidence.

genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local dry skin, allergy, sun sensitivity, or treatment needs.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample summary table uses AQP3 in Hydration and Moisture.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local hydration, lubrication, or skincare response.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample summary table uses AGER in Skin Glycation.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local glycation, wrinkles, radiance, or diet/skincare actionability.

genotypeSummary.effect: The sample summary table uses these genes in Skin Inflammation.

genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local inflammation, allergy, dermatology, or immune status.

genotypeSummary.phenotype: Observed sample content only; do not infer local eyelid sagging, skin aging, or cosmetic outcome.

genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture supplies HERC2/OCA2 visible-trait context for validation.
genotypeSummary.phenotype: Report as educational pigmentation or eye-color context only; do not turn it into exact appearance, identity, ancestry, or medical interpretation.

genotypeSummary.effect: The local fixture supplies MC1R visible-trait context for validation.

missingInputs: Marketplace detail-page fields beyond the public sample PDF have not been extracted.
No calibrated skin-trait probability model is supplied.
Skin type, UV sensitivity, skin aging, acne, skincare product response, dermatology outcomes, and skin-cancer risk require separate validated evidence and are not inferred.
Many known and unknown contributors to pigmentation, freckles, hair texture, and other visible traits are not represented in this fixture.
uncertainty: Visible traits can be influenced by multiple genes, development, age, environment, and other non-genetic factors.
Individual visible-trait markers should not be treated as deterministic appearance predictions.
Public sample rows are examples from a sample report and may not represent the local user.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.

The report is educational and does not identify a person, infer race or ancestry, predict a child's appearance, give skincare guidance, diagnose skin conditions, or report melanoma or other skin-cancer risk.
The Skin Genes public sample PDF is source-bound, but authenticated marketplace detail-page parity remains pending.
